page_content:
BMGT476 Technology Applications in Supply Chain Management (3 Credits) An understanding of the role of technology in managing the supply chain. Provides students with hands-on experience in advanced software systems that build on top of enterprise resource planning systems. Major emphasis is placed on demonstrating that these systems result in supply chain cost reductions and service improvements. Prerequisite: BMGT372.

BMGT477 International Supply Chain Management (3 Credits) The study of the importance of the supply chain management within a global context. Topics covered include: the structure, service, pricing and competitive relationships among international carriers and transport intermediaries as well as documentation, location decisions, international sourcing/distribution and management of inventory throughout the international supply chain.

BMGT478 Special Topics in Supply Chain Management (3 Credits) Selected advanced topics in supply chain management. Repeatable to: 9 credits if content differs. Additional Information: Course prerequisites will vary depending on the topic. A maximum of 3 credits of BMGT478 course work can fulﬁll Supply Chain Management major requirements.
